objective: Remediate critical runtime failures covering gateway 502s, Kafka envelope/ack handling, topic drift, suppression service auth, and local SMTP provider bootstrap.
filesInScope: focused tests and durable memory updates
evidenceBeforeEdit: docker compose ps showed no running local stack, so remediation starts from source and compose inspection before runtime validation.
Nginx routing used named upstreams with service names; route validator required the same static upstream pattern.
Kafka consumers deserialize outer values as EventEnvelope but affected consumers did not use manual acknowledgments.
docker-compose kafka-setup used create-if-missing only and could not repair partition drift for existing topics.
delivery-service local compose did not enable explicit default MailHog provider creation for new tenants.
validationPlan: Run focused Maven tests for Kafka config, topic config, affected consumers, provider selection, and runtime guard.
Run route-map validator fixture tests and repository route-map validation.
Run docker compose config and env validation.
Start the local dockerized stack, validate gateway API routes, Kafka consumer startup, topic partition alignment, container recreation, and browser flow.
rollbackNotes: Revert only files changed by this remediation if validation exposes an unfixable regression; do not revert unrelated dirty files.
nextAction: Run Codex validation, route validation, Compose startup, browser traversal, and targeted fixes until local workflows are clean or blocked by missing credentials/evidence.
